title: Tandoori Chicken Charcoal Grilled

seo.imageCaption: Experience the smoky flavors of India with our homemade Tandoori Chicken Charcoal Grilled.

description: Experience the iconic flavors of India with this authentic Tandoori Chicken, marinated in a rich blend of yogurt and aromatic spices, then perfectly grilled over hot charcoal. The smoky char and tender, juicy meat make it a spectacular centerpiece for any Indian dinner. Prepare to impress with this restaurant-quality dish made right in your backyard.

ingredients.notes: cut into 8-10 pieces, skin removed, deep gashes made

instructions.title: Prepare the Chicken
instructions.text: Wash and pat dry the chicken pieces thoroughly. Make deep gashes all over the chicken with a sharp knife. In a large bowl, combine the chicken with 1 tablespoon lemon juice and 1/2 teaspoon salt. Rub well and let it rest for 10 minutes.

instructions.title: First Marinade
instructions.text: Add ginger garlic paste, red chili powder, and turmeric powder to the chicken. Rub this marinade thoroughly into the chicken pieces, ensuring it gets into the gashes. Let it marinate for at least 30 minutes at room temperature, or up to 2 hours in the refrigerator.

instructions.title: Second Marinade
instructions.text: In a separate bowl, whisk together the yogurt, remaining 2 tablespoons lemon juice, 1/2 teaspoon salt, cumin powder, coriander powder, garam masala, mustard oil, crushed fenugreek leaves, and optional food color. Add the chicken pieces to this yogurt mixture, ensuring they are fully coated. Marinate for a minimum of 4 hours, preferably overnight in the refrigerator, for best flavor.

instructions.title: Prepare the Charcoal Grill
instructions.text: Light your charcoal grill and allow the coals to heat up until they are glowing red and covered with a thin layer of grey ash. Arrange the coals for direct and indirect heat zones. Clean the grill grates and lightly oil them to prevent sticking.

instructions.title: Grill the Chicken
instructions.text: Place the marinated chicken pieces directly over the hot coals (direct heat) for 5-7 minutes per side, turning frequently, until they get a nice char. Then, move the chicken to the indirect heat zone, close the lid, and cook for another 20-25 minutes, or until the internal temperature reaches 165°F (74°C). Baste with melted butter or ghee during the last 10 minutes of cooking.

instructions.title: Rest and Serve
instructions.text: Once cooked, remove the Tandoori Chicken from the grill and let it rest for 5 minutes before serving. This allows the juices to redistribute, keeping the chicken moist. Garnish with fresh lemon wedges and serve hot with mint chutney, sliced onions, or naan bread.

tips: For truly authentic Tandoori flavor, ensure a long marination time, preferably overnight. This allows the spices to penetrate deeply.
Using thick yogurt (like Greek yogurt) is crucial as it clings better to the chicken and prevents the marinade from becoming watery.
Basting the chicken with melted butter or ghee in the final stage of grilling adds a rich flavor and helps keep the chicken moist and tender.
article:
Tandoori Chicken Charcoal Grilled: An Authentic Indian Dinner Delight

There are few dishes that capture the essence of Indian cuisine quite like Tandoori Chicken. Its vibrant color, smoky aroma, and succulent texture make it a perennial favorite across the globe. While traditionally cooked in a clay tandoor oven, recreating this magic at home with a charcoal grill brings an unparalleled depth of flavor, making it a perfect center-piece for any Indian dinner recipes repertoire. Imagine perfectly charred chicken, infused with aromatic spices and a hint of smoke – a true culinary masterpiece that’s surprisingly achievable in your backyard.

## What is Tandoori Chicken Charcoal Grilled?

Tandoori Chicken is a classic Indian dish featuring chicken marinated in a mixture of yogurt and a medley of spices, then traditionally cooked at high heat in a tandoor, a cylindrical clay oven. The yogurt acts as a tenderizer, while the spices infuse the chicken with an incredible depth of flavor. When prepared on a charcoal grill, we aim to replicate that intense, dry heat and the distinctive smoky char that a tandoor provides. The result is chicken that’s crispy and slightly charred on the outside, yet incredibly moist and juicy on the inside, bursting with a complex blend of savory, tangy, and subtly spicy notes.

The marinade is key to authentic Tandoori Chicken. It typically includes ginger, garlic, lemon juice, and a specific blend of ground spices like cumin, coriander, turmeric, and a generous amount of Kashmiri chili powder, which gives the dish its characteristic red or orange hue without adding excessive heat. Some recipes also include a touch of red food coloring to achieve the bright, restaurant-style appearance. The charcoal grilling process adds an extra dimension, imparting a beautiful smokiness that complements the spiced marinade, elevating this dish from delicious to truly unforgettable.

## Ingredients You Need

Crafting the perfect Tandoori Chicken on your charcoal grill starts with selecting fresh, quality ingredients. Here's what you'll need:

*   **For the Chicken:**
    *   1.5-2 kg (3-4 lbs) whole chicken, cut into 8 pieces, or 8-10 bone-in, skinless chicken thighs and drumsticks. Scoring the chicken deeply with a knife helps the marinade penetrate.
*   **For the Marinade:**
    *   1 cup (240g) full-fat plain yogurt (ensure it's thick; drain excess whey if needed).
    *   2 tablespoons ginger-garlic paste (equal parts fresh ginger and garlic, ground).
    *   2 tablespoons lemon juice.
    *   2 teaspoons Kashmiri chili powder (for color and mild heat; substitute with paprika for less heat, or regular chili powder for more).
    *   1 teaspoon turmeric powder.
    *   1 teaspoon ground cumin.
    *   1 teaspoon ground coriander.
    *   1 teaspoon garam masala.
    *   1 teaspoon salt, or to taste.
    *   1/2 teaspoon black pepper.
    *   1-2 drops red food coloring (optional, for vibrant color).
    *   2 tablespoons mustard oil (traditional for a pungent kick) or vegetable oil.
*   **For Basting (optional):**
    *   Melted butter or oil.
    *   Lemon wedges and fresh cilantro for garnish.

## Step-by-Step Instructions

Follow these steps to create succulent Tandoori Chicken on your charcoal grill:

1.  **Prepare the Chicken:** Wash the chicken pieces thoroughly and pat them completely dry with paper towels. Using a sharp knife, make 2-3 deep slits into each piece of chicken. This allows the marinade to deeply penetrate the meat, ensuring maximum flavor and tenderness.
2.  **Make the Marinade:** In a large bowl, combine the yogurt, ginger-garlic paste, lemon juice, Kashmiri chili powder, turmeric, cumin, coriander, garam masala, salt, black pepper, and optional red food coloring. Mix everything thoroughly until you have a smooth, well-combined paste. Stir in the mustard oil or vegetable oil.
3.  **Marinate the Chicken:** Add the scored chicken pieces to the marinade bowl. Using your hands, ensure every piece is thoroughly coated with the marinade, working it into the slits. Cover the bowl and refrigerate for at least 4 hours, or ideally overnight (12-24 hours) for the best flavor and tenderness.
4.  **Prepare the Grill:** About 30-45 minutes before you plan to grill, prepare your charcoal grill. Arrange the charcoal for two-zone cooking: pile charcoal on one side for direct, high heat, and leave the other side empty for indirect heat. Aim for a medium-high temperature (around 375-400°F or 190-200°C). Clean the grill grates and oil them to prevent sticking.
5.  **Grill the Chicken:** If using skewers, thread the marinated chicken pieces onto metal skewers. Place the chicken directly over the hot coals for 5-7 minutes per side, turning frequently, until you achieve a beautiful char and some crispiness. Be careful not to burn it.
6.  **Cook Through:** Once charred, move the chicken to the indirect heat side of the grill. Close the lid and continue cooking for another 20-30 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through and reaches an internal temperature of 165°F (74°C) at its thickest part. Baste occasionally with melted butter or oil during this stage to keep it moist and add flavor.
7.  **Rest and Serve:** Once cooked, remove the Tandoori Chicken from the grill and let it rest for 5-10 minutes. This allows the juices to redistribute, ensuring a tender and moist result. Garnish with fresh cilantro and lemon wedges before serving.

## Pro Tips for the Best Results

Achieving restaurant-quality Tandoori Chicken at home requires attention to a few key details:

*   **Marination is Key:** Don't skimp on marination time. The longer the chicken sits in the yogurt-spice mixture, the more tender and flavorful it will become. Overnight is truly best.
*   **Thick Yogurt:** Use full-fat, plain yogurt. If your yogurt is watery, strain it through a cheesecloth for an hour or two to remove excess whey. This ensures the marinade adheres better to the chicken.
*   **Scoring the Chicken:** Deep slits are crucial. They allow the marinade to penetrate the meat thoroughly, infusing flavor and helping the chicken cook evenly.
*   **Two-Zone Grilling:** This technique is essential for charcoal grilling Tandoori Chicken. Starting with direct heat creates the char, while finishing on indirect heat ensures the chicken cooks through without burning the exterior.
*   **Don't Overcrowd the Grill:** Cook in batches if necessary to maintain consistent grill temperature and allow for even cooking.
*   **Basting:** While optional, a light basting with melted butter or oil during the indirect cooking phase adds richness and helps keep the chicken moist.
*   **Internal Temperature:** Always use a meat thermometer to ensure the chicken is safely cooked to 165°F (74°C). This prevents overcooking and keeps the chicken juicy.
*   **Quality Charcoal:** Use good quality lump charcoal for a cleaner burn and better smoky flavor compared to briquettes.

## Serving Suggestions

Tandoori Chicken is a versatile dish that pairs wonderfully with a variety of accompaniments, making it a fantastic Indian dinner recipe.

*   **Classic Indian Breads:** Serve hot with freshly made naan bread, roti, or paratha to scoop up the delicious juices.
*   **Cooling Chutneys:** A refreshing mint-coriander chutney or a simple raita (yogurt with cucumber and spices) provides a cooling contrast to the spiced chicken.
*   **Fresh Salad:** A simple kachumber salad (diced cucumber, tomato, onion with lemon juice) adds a fresh, crisp element.
*   **Rice Dishes:** Basmati rice, jeera rice (cumin rice), or a light vegetable pulao are excellent choices for a more substantial meal.
*   **Lemon and Onion:** Always serve with plenty of fresh lemon wedges for squeezing over the chicken and thinly sliced raw red onions for a sharp bite.
*   **As an Appetizer:** Cut into smaller pieces, Tandoori Chicken can also be served as a flavorful starter.

## Variations and Substitutions

While the classic Tandoori Chicken is universally loved, there are many ways to adapt this recipe to suit different tastes and dietary needs:

*   **Boneless Options:** For quicker cooking and easier eating, use boneless, skinless chicken thighs or breasts. Adjust grilling time accordingly, as they will cook faster.
*   **Vegetarian Tandoori:** The marinade works beautifully with paneer (Indian cheese), large mushrooms, cauliflower florets, or chunky potatoes. Marinate and grill until tender and charred.
*   **Spice Level:** Adjust the amount of Kashmiri chili powder to control the heat. For a spicier kick, add a pinch of cayenne pepper or finely chopped green chilies to the marinade.
*   **Dairy-Free:** For a dairy-free version, use a plant-based yogurt (like coconut or almond yogurt) or omit the yogurt altogether and increase the lemon juice and oil, relying more on the acidity for tenderization.
*   **Oven or Gas Grill:** If a charcoal grill isn't available, you can achieve good results in a hot oven (broil setting) or on a gas grill. For a gas grill, you can use a smoker box with wood chips to impart some smoky flavor.
*   **Marinade Enhancements:** Experiment with adding a pinch of dried fenugreek leaves (kasuri methi) for an authentic, slightly bitter aroma, or a touch of black salt for a unique tang.

Tandoori Chicken, charcoal grilled to perfection, is more than just a dish; it's an experience. The effort put into marinating and grilling is richly rewarded with incredible flavors and textures that make it a truly special Indian dinner recipe. So fire up your grill, gather your ingredients, and prepare to impress your family and friends with this timeless classic.
